verbatim_text: But it's also quite brittle right so if we simply change the second sentence to later the book was returned to then we actually want to predict Janet and not Jack. So alternate more robust mechanism is to actually use a latent world model to mediate prediction like the POMDP we saw earlier. And so we actually model the state of the world where Jack now possesses a book and not Janet so the book needs to be returned to Janet. Of course these two mechanisms might coexist but what we care about is whether language models do the second mechanism at all.
